> Patch Set 1:
> 
> Many of the headers like api.h is actually a kernel header. So it should go into include/linux/lnet instead

I think everything in include/lnet/ is a kernel header. But I can move them.

This breaks the ability to build/package lnet as a separate module from Lustre, which AFAIR Cray/HPE does for some of their systems (DVFS?).

> This breaks the ability to build/package lnet as a separate module from Lustre, which AFAIR Cray/HPE does for some of their systems (DVFS?).

That should still be possible, even after moving the headers. Of course, I probably didn't adjust the packaging correctly. Still need to debug that.

parents.subject: LU-19179 build: remove references to HAVE_IOP_SET_ACL

commit.subject: LU-18687 lnet: move include to common directory
commit.message:
LU-18687 lnet: move include to common directory

Move the LNET headers to the top-level. This will simplify the
compiler command-line, isolate autotools dependencies to the
headers, and allow for LNET to eventually use plain Makefiles.

Test-Parameters: trivial
Signed-off-by: Timothy Day <timday@amazon.com>
Change-Id: I8c6bfd193e6275428b7b2aa38e15a50a1f7f0c04
